title: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.title: Authentic Patbingsu Recipe: Korean Red Bean Shaved Ice
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Patbingsu, the refreshing Korean red bean shaved ice dessert, at home! This easy recipe brings the taste of Korea to your kitchen. Perfect for summer!

easy Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice recipe
authentic Korean Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

best Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice at home

seo.h1: Authentic Patbingsu: Korean Red Bean Shaved Ice Recipe
h2Headings: What is Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice?
Pro Tips for the Best Patbingsu Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of homemade Patbingsu, Korean red bean shaved ice, topped with sweet red beans, mochi, and condensed milk.
seo.imageCaption: Cool down with a refreshing bowl of Patbingsu, the iconic Korean red bean shaved ice dessert, perfect for a hot day!

description: Patbingsu is a beloved Korean shaved ice dessert, perfect for cooling down on a hot day. This refreshing treat features finely shaved ice topped with sweet red beans, chewy rice cakes, condensed milk, and various fruits. It offers a delightful balance of textures and flavors, making it a popular choice across all ages.

ingredients.notes: or to taste, for red beans

instructions.title: Prepare Sweet Red Beans (Pat)
instructions.text: Rinse the dried red beans thoroughly. In a pot, cover beans with plenty of water and bring to a boil for 10 minutes. Drain the water, then add fresh water and simmer for 30-40 minutes until beans are tender but not mushy. Drain most of the water, leaving just enough to cover the beans, then stir in sugar until dissolved and beans are sweet. Let cool completely.

instructions.title: Prepare Milk Ice Base
instructions.text: Pour the milk into a shallow freezer-safe container or ice cube trays. Freeze for at least 4-6 hours, or overnight, until completely solid. If using a block, ensure it is thick enough for shaving.

instructions.title: Prepare Toppings
instructions.text: If using mini rice cakes, boil them until soft and chewy, then drain and cool. Slice fresh fruits like strawberries or mango into bite-sized pieces. Gather all other toppings like condensed milk and Injeolmi powder.

instructions.title: Shave the Ice
instructions.text: Using an electric ice shaver or a manual shaver, shave the frozen milk into fine, fluffy snow-like ice. If you do not have a shaver, you can blend the frozen milk cubes in a powerful blender until a fine texture is achieved, working in batches.

instructions.title: Assemble the Patbingsu
instructions.text: Divide the shaved ice evenly into individual serving bowls. Create a slight mound in the center of each bowl. Spoon a generous amount of the prepared sweet red beans over the shaved ice.

instructions.title: Add Final Toppings and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the cooked rice cakes and fresh fruit around the sweet red beans. Drizzle generously with condensed milk. Optionally, sprinkle with Injeolmi powder for an authentic touch. Serve immediately to enjoy its refreshing coolness.

tips: For the fluffiest shaved ice, freeze milk in a shallow pan rather than ice cube trays. This creates a larger block that shaves more evenly.
Adjust the sweetness of the red beans and the amount of condensed milk to your personal preference. Some prefer a less sweet base.
Serve Patbingsu immediately after assembly to prevent the ice from melting too quickly. A chilled bowl can also help maintain its frosty texture.
article: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice is a beloved Korean Desserts dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
